title: Hill Conjecture
statement_text: The minimum number of crossings in any drawing of the complete graph $K_n$ in the plane is $$H(n) = \frac{1}{4} \left\lfloor \frac{n}{2} \right\rfloor \left\lfloor \frac{n-1}{2} \right\rfloor \left\lfloor \frac{n-2}{2} \right\rfloor \left\lfloor \frac{n-3}{2} \right\rfloor = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{64} n(n-2)^2(n-4), & n \text{ even};\\ \frac{1}{64}(n-1)^2(n-3)^2, & n \text{ odd.} \end{cases}$$
context_text: In the 1950s, English painter Anthony Hill described drawings of $K_n$ in the plane with exactly $H(n)$ crossings; it became a conjecture that this number is the minimum possible. Despite serious efforts the conjecture remains widely open. The only previous classes of Hill drawings were the cylindrical drawings and the 2-page drawings of Blažek–Koman (1963); it has been proved that neither class can beat $H(n)$.
paper_contribution: Provides the first very general construction of drawings of $K_n$ attaining exactly $H(n)$ crossings via antipodal geodesic drawings on the unit sphere $S^2$, gives a 'book proof' that each such drawing achieves $H(n)$, and derives as a corollary that $\mathrm{cr}_{S^2}(K_n) \leq H(n)$; also gives a simple explanation of Moon's 1968 asymptotic result that random geodesic drawings approach $H(n)$.
notes: PDF source; formula notation (floor functions) is readable and consistent with the known Hill formula.
paper_title: On a conjecture by Anthony Hill

title: Conjecture 5
statement_text: The crossing number of $M_{n,t}$ is equal to $$H(n) - \frac{1}{2}\,t(k-1)(k-2),$$ where $k = \lfloor n/2 \rfloor$.
context_text: Here $M_{n,t}$ denotes the graph obtained from $K_n$ by removing a matching of size $t$, and $k = \lfloor n/2 \rfloor$. Corollary 4 shows that geodesic drawings of $M_{n,t}$ built from sets of strength $0$ achieve this crossing count; the existence of many such sets of strength $0$ motivates the conjecture that this value is optimal.
notes: PDF source; the algebraic expression is unambiguous.
paper_title: On a conjecture by Anthony Hill
